source_url.status: CONFIRMED — resolves to Directive 2014/35/EU (Low Voltage Directive) on EUR-Lex. Canonical CELEX form is correct.

standard_version_error.issue: EN IEC 62368-1:2020+A11:2020 (3rd edition) was published by CENELEC but was NEVER cited in the EU Official Journal as a harmonised standard under LVD 2014/35/EU. The 3rd edition failed to achieve OJEU harmonised status due to HAS consultative objections from member states. The currently cited harmonised standard in the OJEU for LVD is EN 62368-1:2014 (2nd edition). The 4th edition of IEC 62368-1 (published May 2023) is expected to eventually replace the 2nd edition in the OJEU, but is not yet harmonised. Citing EN IEC 62368-1:2020+A11:2020 as the harmonised standard is a material regulatory error — manufacturers relying on it do not have a presumption of conformity under LVD.
standard_version_error.corrected_standard: EN 62368-1:2014 (harmonised under LVD 2014/35/EU — 2nd edition; currently cited in OJEU). Note: EN IEC 62368-1:2020+A11:2020 (3rd edition) exists and is technically superior but is NOT harmonised under LVD and does NOT confer presumption of conformity. Manufacturers using the 3rd edition must demonstrate conformity with LVD essential requirements by other means.
standard_version_error.action: UPDATE all three language versions of target_requirement.summary and standards_or_laws to cite EN 62368-1:2014 as the OJEU-harmonised standard, with a note that the 2020/3rd edition is not harmonised.

cn_standard_version_error.issue: GB 4943.1-2022 is based on IEC 62368-1:2018 (2nd edition published 2018), not IEC 62368-1:2020. Multiple authoritative sources (UL, TUV SUD, SIQ, SAMR documentation) confirm the base is IEC 62368-1:2018. Describing it as technically equivalent to IEC 62368-1:2020 overstates the correspondence.
cn_standard_version_error.corrected_text_en: GB 4943.1-2022 is technically based on IEC 62368-1:2018 (not IEC 62368-1:2020) with additional national differences (stricter overcurrent protection, isolation requirements, high-temperature/humidity insulation provisions). It replaced GB 4943.1-2011 and GB 8898-2011. Implementation: new CCC applications required from 1 August 2023; existing certificates to be updated by 31 July 2024.
cn_standard_version_error.action: CORRECT all three language versions of cn_common_equivalent.summary to state IEC 62368-1:2018 (not 2020) as the base. Update standards_or_laws entry to read 'GB 4943.1-2022 (based on IEC 62368-1:2018 with national deviations; mandatory; replaces GB 4943.1-2011 and GB 8898-2011)'.

gap_note: Gap analysis is substantively correct: CCC and CE are parallel mandatory regimes with no mutual recognition. The need for EU-specific testing (including A11 annex requirements) and fresh DoC is accurately stated. Minor note: the A11 annex reference should be updated once the harmonised standard citation is corrected.
note: LVD 2014/35/EU confirmed mandatory for EU market. CCC mandatory for CN market. Key correction: the harmonised safety standard currently cited in the OJEU under LVD is EN 62368-1:2014 (2nd edition), not EN IEC 62368-1:2020+A11:2020. The 2020 3rd edition is not harmonised. GB 4943.1-2022 is based on IEC 62368-1:2018, not 2020.

source_url.status: CONFIRMED — resolves to LVD 2014/35/EU on EUR-Lex.

note: CE/DoC self-declaration model vs CCC third-party model is correctly described. LVD Articles 7 and 17 on DoC and CE marking are correctly cited. Decision 768/2008/EC (NLF Module A) and Regulation (EC) 765/2008 references are correct. 10-year retention period is correct. The description of CCC as requiring a CNCA-designated body is correct. No errors found in this row.

source_url.status: CONFIRMED — ELI permalink for EMC Directive 2014/30/EU. Published OJ L 96, 29.3.2014. Resolves correctly.

cn_mandatory_status_clarification.issue: GB/T 9254.1-2021 and GB/T 9254.2-2021 carry GB/T (recommended) designation by standard type, but they are referenced as mandatory test standards within the CCC certification scheme for ITE/AV/telecom products under CNCA. Effective 1 July 2022, all new CCC applications for ITE/multimedia equipment must use these standards; CCC certification under the new standards is mandatory for those products. The fragment's characterisation of these as 'recommended (GB/T) — not legally compulsory as standalone requirements' is partially correct in that the standards themselves carry a recommended designation, but this understates the practical mandatory effect via CCC. The UNVERIFIED qualification is appropriate to retain.
cn_mandatory_status_clarification.action: ADD clarifying note to mandatory_status.cn: 'GB/T 9254.1-2021 and GB/T 9254.2-2021 carry the GB/T (recommended) designation, but are referenced as mandatory test standards within the CCC scheme for ITE/multimedia equipment from 1 July 2022 onward — the practical enforcement is via CCC, not direct standalone mandate.' No other text changes needed; the UNVERIFIED flags are appropriate.

ecodesign_successor_note.field: N/A (not in this fragment but relevant context)
ecodesign_successor_note.issue: No error in this row. Informational only.

note: EMC Directive 2014/30/EU source confirmed. EN 55032, EN 55035, EN IEC 61000-3-2 are correctly identified as harmonised standards under 2014/30/EU. The UNVERIFIED qualifier on OJ citation dates for EN 55032:2015/A11:2020 and EN 55035:2017/A11:2020 is appropriate and should be retained. Key correction: GB/T 9254.1/9254.2 mandatory-vs-recommended status needs clarification — they are GB/T (recommended by type) but mandated via CCC for relevant product categories from July 2022. Gap analysis is substantively accurate on re-issuance requirement and harmonic standard edition lag.

source_url.status: CONFIRMED — ELI permalink for EMC Directive 2014/30/EU. Resolves correctly.

note: GaN high-frequency switching EMI characterisation is technically accurate. EN 55032 conducted emission window (150 kHz–30 MHz), Class B limits, and the asymmetry above/below 500 kHz are correctly described. The UNVERIFIED qualifier on specific dBuV figures and GaN switching frequency ranges is appropriate. The CN framework description is accurate: no GaN-specific standard exists; enforcement is via CCC/GB 4343.1 depending on product classification. Re-issuance requirement is correctly stated. No factual errors found.

source_url.status: CONFIRMED — resolves to Commission Regulation (EU) 2019/1782. Preferred canonical form is the ELI permalink: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2019/1782/oj/eng
source_url.action: UPDATE source.url to ELI permalink for consistency with other rows.

ecodesign_successor_regulation.issue: Regulation (EU) 2019/1782 will be repealed and replaced by Regulation (EU) 2025/2052 (adopted 13 October 2025, OJ L 2025/2052) with application from 14 December 2028. The successor regulation expands scope to include wireless chargers, wireless charging pads, universal portable battery chargers, and USB Type-C cables. It also mandates USB-C or USB-PD port on all EPS by default and introduces a 'Common Charger' logo. Products placed on market between 14 December 2025 and 14 December 2028 complying with 2025/2052 are deemed compliant with 2019/1782. The fragment does not mention this upcoming transition.
ecodesign_successor_regulation.action: ADD a note in target_requirement.summary (all three languages) that Regulation (EU) 2019/1782 is in force until 14 December 2028 when it is repealed by Regulation (EU) 2025/2052, and that manufacturers should monitor transition requirements from 14 December 2025 onward. ADD 'Regulation (EU) 2025/2052 (successor — applies from 14 December 2028; EUR-Lex: https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2025/2052/oj/eng)' to standards_or_laws.

cn_standard_update.issue: The fragment references GB 20943 without specifying version. The current mandatory CN standard is GB 20943-2013 (in force as of 2026). A revised standard GB 20943-2025 was published 24 January 2025 with mandatory enforcement from 1 February 2027, broadening scope to include internal power supplies and raising efficiency grade requirements.
cn_standard_update.action: UPDATE standards_or_laws to 'GB 20943-2013 (current mandatory; minimum allowable values of energy efficiency and energy efficiency grades for single-phase single-output external power supplies)' and ADD note: 'GB 20943-2025 published 24 January 2025, mandatory enforcement from 1 February 2027 — expanded scope (includes internal supplies) and raised efficiency thresholds.'

note: Ecodesign EPS regulation (EU) 2019/1782 confirmed. Tier 2 requirements from 1 April 2020 confirmed. <=250 W scope and EPS product definition are correct. Key corrections: (1) source.url updated to ELI permalink; (2) successor Regulation (EU) 2025/2052 (December 2028) should be noted; (3) GB 20943 reference should specify version — current is GB 20943-2013; GB 20943-2025 published Jan 2025, mandatory from Feb 2027. Gap analysis on efficiency threshold, no-load, labelling, and test method differences is accurate.

source_url.status: CONFIRMED — resolves to Directive (EU) 2022/2380 (Common Charger amendment to RED). CELEX form is correct.

laptop_deadline_confirmed.status: CONFIRMED — 28 December 2024 for most portable electronics (phones, tablets, cameras, headphones, etc.) and 28 April 2026 for laptops are both confirmed by the European Commission and multiple sources.

ecodesign_2025_2052_link.issue: Regulation (EU) 2025/2052 (December 2028) mandates that ALL new EPS must carry at least one USB-C or USB-PD port by default, further entrenching the common-charger interface requirement at the EPS level independent of RED 2022/2380.
ecodesign_2025_2052_link.action: OPTIONAL: note in summary that Ecodesign Regulation (EU) 2025/2052 reinforces USB-C/USB-PD requirement on EPS from December 2028.

note: Common Charger Directive (EU) 2022/2380 confirmed. Dates confirmed: 28 December 2024 (phones/tablets/peripherals) and 28 April 2026 (laptops). USB-C + USB-PD requirement confirmed. EN IEC 62680-1-3:2021 is the correct harmonised standard cited. The CN gap (no equivalent mandatory common-charger law) is accurate. Gap on proprietary-only fast-charge protocols is accurate. No errors. Informational note: Regulation (EU) 2025/2052 further mandates USB-C/USB-PD on all EPS from December 2028.

source_url.status: CONFIRMED — resolves to Regulation (EU) 2019/1020 on Market Surveillance. Correct.
source_url.secondary_url_note: The publisher field mentions Directive 2022/2380 URL https://eur-lex.europa.eu/legal-content/EN/TXT/?uri=CELEX%3A32022L2380 as a secondary reference — also confirmed.

directive_number_typo.issue: The summary text cites 'Directive 2022/2380/EU' — the correct citation format is 'Directive (EU) 2022/2380' (EU number first per EUR-Lex style). The standards_or_laws list has 'Directive 2022/2380/EU' which is a non-standard citation format. This is a minor formatting issue rather than a factual error.
directive_number_typo.action: UPDATE all instances of '2022/2380/EU' to '(EU) 2022/2380' in all three language versions for citation consistency.

ecodesign_successor_note.issue: Regulation (EU) 2019/1782 is listed in standards_or_laws as 'Ecodesign Regulation (EU) 2019/1782 or its successor for external power supplies' — the successor is now confirmed as Regulation (EU) 2025/2052 (applies December 2028). The placeholder 'or its successor' language in the original can be made concrete.
ecodesign_successor_note.action: UPDATE 'or its successor' to '(to be replaced by Regulation (EU) 2025/2052 from 14 December 2028)' in standards_or_laws.

common_charger_logo_requirement.issue: Regulation (EU) 2025/2052 (successor Ecodesign) introduces a mandatory Common Charger logo on EPS from December 2028, distinct from the existing CE mark. This is not a correction to the current fragment (which covers current rules) but may need future update.
common_charger_logo_requirement.action: OPTIONAL future update when 2025/2052 transition nears.

note: Market access overview is substantively correct. CE bundle requirements (LVD + EMC + Ecodesign + RED if wireless), common charger mandate, EU responsible person (Regulation 2019/1020), WEEE registration, RoHS substance restrictions are all correctly identified. Minor fixes: (1) citation format '2022/2380/EU' should be '(EU) 2022/2380'; (2) confirm ecodesign successor (Regulation (EU) 2025/2052, December 2028). Gap analysis is accurate. The 3-6 month planning estimate is reasonable.
